# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instances with attached EBS volumes for application data (phase 1 quick-start)

Parameters:
  EnvironmentName:
    Type: String
    Default: dev
    Description: Environment name prefix

  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    Description: EC2 instance type

  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: Optional SSH key pair name for access

  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC where instances will be launched

  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: Subnet where instances will be launched

  ApplicationPort:
    Type: Number
    Default: 8080
    Description: Application listener port

  RootVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 30
    Description: Root EBS volume size in GiB

  DataVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 100
    Description: Data EBS volume size in GiB

  InstanceCount:
    Type: Number
    Default: 2
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 10
    Description: Number of EC2 instances to launch

  AllowedCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
    Description: CIDR allowed to access the application port

Mappings:
  AMIRegionMap:
    us-east-1:
      AMI: ami-0c02fb55956c7d316
    us-east-2:
      AMI: ami-024e6efaf93d85776
    us-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0b2f6494ff0b07a0e
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-0892d3c7ee96c0bf7
    eu-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0a8e758f5e873d1c1
    eu-central-1:
      AMI: ami-0fa1de1d60de6a97e
    ap-southeast-1:
      AMI: ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0
    ap-southeast-2:
      AMI: ami-09b42976632b27e9b
    ap-northeast-1:
      AMI: ami-0d527b8c289b4af7f
    ap-northeast-2:
      AMI: ami-0f2ed5f5b0f9a9a2e

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for application EC2 instances
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: !Ref ApplicationPort
          ToPort: !Ref ApplicationPort
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowedCidr
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-sg'

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-ec2-role'

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  LaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: !FindInMap [AMIRegionMap, !Ref 'AWS::Region', AMI]
        InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
        KeyName: !Ref KeyName
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Arn: !GetAtt InstanceProfile.Arn
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
        BlockDeviceMappings:
          - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
            Ebs:
              VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
              VolumeType: gp3
              DeleteOnTermination: true
              Encrypted: false
          - DeviceName: /dev/sdf
            Ebs:
              VolumeSize: !Ref DataVolumeSize
              VolumeType: gp3
              DeleteOnTermination: false
              Encrypted: false
        UserData:
          Fn::Base64: !Sub |
            #!/bin/bash -xe
            yum update -y
            mkdir -p /app/data
            mkfs -t xfs /dev/xvdf || true
            grep -qs '/app/data' /etc/fstab || echo '/dev/xvdf /app/data xfs defaults,nofail 0 2' >> /etc/fstab
            mount -a
            echo "Application host $(hostname)" > /app/data/hostname.txt

  EC2Instance1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref LaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt LaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-1'

  EC2Instance2:
    Condition: HasSecondInstance
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref LaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt LaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-2'

Conditions:
  HasSecondInstance: !Not [!Equals [!Ref InstanceCount, 1]]

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: Security group ID
    Value: !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup

  InstanceProfileName:
    Description: IAM instance profile name
    Value: !Ref InstanceProfile

  Instance1Id:
    Description: First EC2 instance ID
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance1

  Instance2Id:
    Condition: HasSecondInstance
    Description: Second EC2 instance ID
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance2
